name: Pixie (eBPF Kubernetes Observability)
what_it_does: eBPF-based Kubernetes observability platform that auto-instruments services without code changes, exposing metrics, traces, and application data via a SQL-like query language (PxL) and REST API.
use_cases: Querying service latency and error rates without instrumenting application code
Detecting anomalies in Kubernetes service-to-service traffic for SRE agents
Profiling CPU and memory usage across pods without any application changes
Tracing HTTP, gRPC, MySQL, and Redis requests automatically via eBPF
Building custom observability dashboards by querying Pixie's data engine programmatically
not_for: Non-Kubernetes deployments (bare metal, VMs without K8s)
Long-term metric storage (Pixie stores ~1 hour of data in-cluster)
Classic APM use cases requiring distributed trace persistence for weeks
best_when: An agent needs zero-instrumentation observability of Kubernetes microservices for performance debugging or anomaly detection.
avoid_when: You need long-term metric retention, non-K8s environments, or traditional trace waterfall views with months of history.

auth.notes: API keys generated from the Pixie cloud console. In-cluster gRPC API also accessible via px CLI auth. New Relic acquired Pixie and continues open-source maintenance.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community edition: fully free, unlimited clusters. Pixie cloud hosted: free for community use
pricing.paid_tiers: Integrated into New Relic One pricing for enterprise support
pricing.notes: Pixie is Apache 2.0 open-source. Deployable entirely in your own cluster. No data leaves the cluster unless explicitly exported. New Relic hosts a free cloud control plane.

performance.rate_limits: Query execution limited by in-cluster resources; no external rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: PxL query language is well-documented. REST API is less documented than the px CLI and Python SDK. Error messages from PxL queries are descriptive when queries fail.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All queries are read-only and fully idempotent. Same PxL query on same data returns identical results.
known_agent_gotchas: Data retention is ~1 hour in-cluster — agents querying historical data beyond 1h will get no results
PxL is a custom SQL-like language — not standard SQL; requires learning the dialect
Requires eBPF kernel support (Linux 4.14+) and Kubernetes 1.16+
Cluster-local data means agents must be deployed in or near the cluster to minimize latency

security_score_components.security_notes: Data stays in-cluster by default — excellent data sovereignty story. eBPF runs with kernel privileges which requires careful security review. Apache 2.0 open-source. New Relic security practices apply to cloud control plane.
